original_hypothesis.title: Auction Address Can Be Set to Zero via Removed AuctionFactory Entries
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In GenesisPoolManager.depositNativeToken(), the auction is selected via auctionFactory.auctions(auctionIndex) with a fallback to auctions(0). If both the requested index and index 0 point to removed (zeroed) entries, the auction is set to address(0) in the genesis pool, causing all subsequent depositToken calls to revert when calling auction methods.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The code: `auction = auctionFactory.auctions(auctionIndex); auction = auction == address(0) ? auctionFactory.auctions(0) : auction;`. If auctionFactory.removeAuction(0) was called, auctions[0] = address(0). Then both branches return address(0). setGenesisPoolInfo stores address(0) as the auction, and _getNativeTokenAmount will revert on any call to IAuction(address(0)).
deployment_assumptions: AuctionFactory owner removes the auction at index 0
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: AuctionFactory.removeAuction() sets auctions[_pos] = address(0); no deployment evidence prevents removal of index 0

proof_of_signal.pattern: auction fallback to auctions(0) which can be address(0) if removed; no null check before setGenesisPoolInfo

preconditions: AuctionFactory owner removes the default auction at index 0
User calls depositNativeToken with an invalid or removed auctionIndex

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No null check on final auction address before use
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: address(0) stored as auction, bricking all deposit operations

analysis_questions: Is index 0 the only fallback? Could another fallback mechanism exist?
Can governance/admin recover by calling setAuction on the affected pool?
What's the blast radius: just one pool or all future pools?
validation_requirements: Remove auction at index 0, then call depositNativeToken
Verify genesis pool stores address(0) as auction

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Admin removes default auction. User deposits native token. Auction set to address(0). All user deposit attempts on this genesis pool revert. Pool becomes permanently non-functional unless governance calls setAuction to fix it.
prerequisites: User creates genesis pool during this window

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Genesis pool bricked; native tokens locked unless pool is rejected
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Token owner and potential depositors of the affected pool
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Governance can call setAuction() to fix the pool

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for removed auction fallback behavior

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
